_README: EDIT THIS FILE TO UPDATE YOUR PORTFOLIO. See README.md for instructions. Anything starting with _ is a comment and ignored by the site.

photos.title: Pokhara, Held in Pink Light
photos.caption: Machapuchare lit up for maybe forty seconds. The rooftops of the city were already in shadow. There's a strange holiness to a mountain that catches light a town can no longer reach.

photos.title: Layered, in Blue
photos.caption: Six ridges, each a slightly paler blue than the one before. The dark roof in the foreground belonged to a teahouse where I'd just had milk tea and watched the sky go from gold to nothing.

photos.title: Under the Tracks
photos.caption: Beneath the Yamanote line, the city becomes something else — pipes and tarps and an old man walking home through a corridor of fluorescent and neon.

photos.title: Van Gogh Vodka
photos.caption: A bar I'll never know the inside of. The lantern was older than the building, probably.

photos.title: Stillwater, Last Light
photos.caption: The lake went mirror-still about four minutes before the sun dropped. Twenty miles from the nearest road and you could hear loons from the next bay over.

photos.title: The Bridge, with Lilies
photos.caption: The dog had claimed the bridge. I waited for it to move and it never did. The lilies were doing their own thing entirely.

photos.title: Pagoda, with Sleeping Dog
photos.caption: The temple was empty. The dog had been there long enough that the staff had stopped trying to move him.

photos.title: Ironworks, Reclaimed
photos.caption: An abandoned brick structure beside a river, with cable cars running overhead. Modernity literally floating above what time forgot.

photos.title: Window Within a Window
photos.caption: I noticed the reflection only after I'd walked past the building. I went back. The geometry repeats — windows behind glass behind windows — for as long as you keep looking.

photos.title: Entering Zion Wilderness
photos.caption: The sign was the only thing in the foreground for a quarter mile. The cliffs behind it had been there for two hundred million years.

photos.title: Road, Returning
photos.caption: A road the desert is patiently un-making. The yellow line was still visible. The grasses had already won.

photos.title: Annapurna Base Camp
photos.caption: Four days walking up. The blue tents in the distance were where we'd sleep. The grass had already turned for winter.

photos.title: Threshold
photos.caption: Hotel Superview. The hiker paused for half a second in the doorway and the prayer flags were still. Behind it all, Machapuchare doing what it does.

photos.title: Light, Through Wires
photos.caption: Crepuscular rays through the storm clouds, telephone wires cutting through them, a small boat full of salt or rice or something white. I stood in the water until the light moved.

photos.title: Fishtail, Final Light
photos.caption: Machapuchare means 'fishtail.' Climbing it is forbidden — the summit is sacred to Shiva. From base camp, you understand why.

photos.title: Crimson and Gold
photos.caption: The flag was new. The mountain was being lit by a sun that had already set on the village below.

photos.title: Nakamise, in Bloom
photos.caption: The same street my grandparents would have walked. The blossoms are a week old and already starting to fall.

photos.title: The Small Shrine
photos.caption: Found just off the path, mostly forgotten. A mesh strainer, a paper tag, a small offering. Whoever tends to it does so when no one is watching.

photos.title: The Tree Above the Stones
photos.caption: A cemetery on a hill above Kyoto. The tower in the distance has been there sixty years; the tree, much longer; the stones, longer still.

photos.title: HOTEL
photos.caption: One street lamp, one neon sign, a thousand wires. The whole block looked like this.

photos.title: Defender, in Lava Field
photos.caption: Someone's expedition didn't end well. The rocks on the dashboard suggested it had been there a while.

photos.title: Frostastaðavatn
photos.caption: The light moved fast here. By the time I'd recomposed, the cloud bank had crossed half the valley.

photos.title: Way Marked
photos.caption: Red poles to keep you on the path when the snow comes.

siteText._comment: Edit these to change the wording on your site
siteText.heroHeadline:
Light, water,
and the long
edges of places.
siteText.heroTagline: Photography from the Annapurnas, the American West, the Boundary Waters, and wherever the light lands quietly.
siteText.workIntro: A working archive — moments collected across continents and seasons, arranged not by place but by feel. Click any image to read its notes.
siteText.seriesIntro: Bodies of work that demanded their own chapter. More to come as the archive grows.
siteText.aboutLine1: I'm Cade. I photograph large expanses and small reclamations — the places where light lands gently and where nature is patiently outlasting whatever we built.
siteText.aboutLine2: The work moves between two registers: the cinematic — alpenglow on Himalayan peaks, golden hour on northern lakes — and the archaeological — moss on stone, brick reclaimed by jungle, the quiet evidence of time. Both, I think, are the same story told twice.
siteText.aboutLine3: Limited edition prints available. Open to editorial, brand, and licensing collaborations.
